title: George Municipal requests to be declared a Water-Scarce Area
bodyText: [{"type":"intro_bold","text":"Level 2D water restrictions and Level 3 emergency tariffs are in effect to reduce consumption and support additional operational costs. A Water Management Task Team monitors dam levels and coordinates interventions."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"GEORGE NEWS - George Municipality informs residents, businesses, and stakeholders that the Council, on Thursday, 29 January, unanimously approved the request for a classification of the George Municipal Area as a Local Disaster Area due to severe water challenges and the current drought conditions.","html":"GEORGE NEWS - George Municipality informs residents, businesses, and stakeholders that the Council, on Thursday, 29 January, unanimously approved the request for a classification of the George Municipal Area as a Local Disaster Area due to severe water challenges and the current drought conditions."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"This request is directed at the Provincial Disaster Management Centre, where the assessment will be conducted."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The municipality is experiencing critical water shortages following prolonged below-average rainfall, which is expected to continue.","html":"The municipality is experiencing critical water shortages following prolonged below-average rainfall, which is expected to continue."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The Garden Route Dam, the main water source for George, stood at 44.77% on 26 January compared to 87.87% last year, while Haarlem Dam, supplying Haarlem and Uniondale, was at 24.00% compared to 85,42% last year.","html":"The Garden Route Dam, the main water source for George, stood at 44.77% on 26 January compared to 87.87% last year, while Haarlem Dam, supplying Haarlem and Uniondale, was at 24.00% compared to 85,42% last year."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Uniondale’s supply from the Kammanassie River is also severely depleted, with limited groundwater available.","html":"Uniondale’s supply from the Kammanassie River is also severely depleted, with limited groundwater available."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"The area is affected by both hydro-meteorological drought, reducing dam, river, and groundwater levels, and agricultural drought, impacting soil moisture, crops, and farming activities."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Level 2D water restrictions and Level 3 emergency tariffs are in effect to reduce consumption and support additional operational costs. A Water Management Task Team monitors dam levels and coordinates interventions.","html":"Level 2D water restrictions and Level 3 emergency tariffs are in effect to reduce consumption and support additional operational costs. A Water Management Task Team monitors dam levels and coordinates interventions."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The disaster declaration allows for stronger coordination with the Garden Route District Municipality and other government spheres, access to relief mechanisms, and implementation of short- and long-term measures to address water scarcity.","html":"The disaster declaration allows for stronger coordination with the Garden Route District Municipality and other government spheres, access to relief mechanisms, and implementation of short- and long-term measures to address water scarcity."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Residents are urged to use water sparingly and comply with all restrictions to preserve available resources during this critical period.","html":"Residents are urged to use water sparingly and comply with all restrictions to preserve available resources during this critical period."}]
bodyHtml: [{"type":"intro_bold","text":"Level 2D water restrictions and Level 3 emergency tariffs are in effect to reduce consumption and support additional operational costs. A Water Management Task Team monitors dam levels and coordinates interventions."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"GEORGE NEWS - George Municipality informs residents, businesses, and stakeholders that the Council, on Thursday, 29 January, unanimously approved the request for a classification of the George Municipal Area as a Local Disaster Area due to severe water challenges and the current drought conditions.","html":"GEORGE NEWS - George Municipality informs residents, businesses, and stakeholders that the Council, on Thursday, 29 January, unanimously approved the request for a classification of the George Municipal Area as a Local Disaster Area due to severe water challenges and the current drought conditions."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"This request is directed at the Provincial Disaster Management Centre, where the assessment will be conducted."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The municipality is experiencing critical water shortages following prolonged below-average rainfall, which is expected to continue.","html":"The municipality is experiencing critical water shortages following prolonged below-average rainfall, which is expected to continue."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The Garden Route Dam, the main water source for George, stood at 44.77% on 26 January compared to 87.87% last year, while Haarlem Dam, supplying Haarlem and Uniondale, was at 24.00% compared to 85,42% last year.","html":"The Garden Route Dam, the main water source for George, stood at 44.77% on 26 January compared to 87.87% last year, while Haarlem Dam, supplying Haarlem and Uniondale, was at 24.00% compared to 85,42% last year."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Uniondale’s supply from the Kammanassie River is also severely depleted, with limited groundwater available.","html":"Uniondale’s supply from the Kammanassie River is also severely depleted, with limited groundwater available."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"The area is affected by both hydro-meteorological drought, reducing dam, river, and groundwater levels, and agricultural drought, impacting soil moisture, crops, and farming activities."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Level 2D water restrictions and Level 3 emergency tariffs are in effect to reduce consumption and support additional operational costs. A Water Management Task Team monitors dam levels and coordinates interventions.","html":"Level 2D water restrictions and Level 3 emergency tariffs are in effect to reduce consumption and support additional operational costs. A Water Management Task Team monitors dam levels and coordinates interventions."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The disaster declaration allows for stronger coordination with the Garden Route District Municipality and other government spheres, access to relief mechanisms, and implementation of short- and long-term measures to address water scarcity.","html":"The disaster declaration allows for stronger coordination with the Garden Route District Municipality and other government spheres, access to relief mechanisms, and implementation of short- and long-term measures to address water scarcity."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Residents are urged to use water sparingly and comply with all restrictions to preserve available resources during this critical period.","html":"Residents are urged to use water sparingly and comply with all restrictions to preserve available resources during this critical period."}]

images.alt: George Municipal requests to be declared a Water-Scarce Area

teaser: Level 2D water restrictions and Level 3 emergency tariffs are in effect to reduce consumption and support additional operational costs. A Water Management Task Team monitors dam levels and coordinates interventions.
featuredCaption: Garden Route Dam. Photo: George Municipality
